title: Dichodon persicum
taxonomy: Dichodon persicum is classified as an extinct genus of coniferous plant. It belongs to the division Pinophyta, which includes all conifers. Further classification places Dichodon persicum within the class Pinopsida, order Pinales, and family Araucariaceae. The genus is thought to have existed during the Early Cretaceous period. Its classification provides insight into the evolution of conifers during this time.

The plant's roots and seeds are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues. Additionally, Dichodon persicum is believed to have antioxidant properties, which can help protect against cell damage and promote overall health.
